bullets.text: Net premiums written grew 19% to $2,912.8 million from $2,457.2 million in 1994, with Core business NPW up 21% to $2,824.2 million; Progressive grew without additional debt or equity financing.
Progressive posted its 23rd underwriting profit in 29 years with a 5.7% margin in 1995, exceeding the 4% target; ROE was 19.6% versus 27.4% in 1994 as margins moderated toward long-term levels.
Underwriting expense ratio fell to 22% in 1995 from 24% in 1994 and 32% in 1990, continuing the multi-year cost reduction drive; Gainsharing represented 9.4% of total employee compensation.

Total investment portfolio reached $3,768.0 million; debt-to-total capital ratio fell to 31% from 37% in 1994; NPW-to-statutory surplus ratio was 2.8:1; equity exposure increased to 8.2% from 3.4% of portfolio.
Progressive launched the first major insurer website on the World Wide Web on April 7, 1995, offering financial data, comparative rates, and product information at auto-insurance.com.

Progressive expanded distribution through 30,000+ independent agencies, telephone service, and joint national account marketing relationships; standard and preferred market grew to 5-10% of total 1995 volume.

Progressive held 2.6% U.S. private passenger auto market share as the 7th largest insurer, increasingly benchmarked against Allstate, GEICO, State Farm, Farmers, USAA, and Nationwide rather than specialty insurers.

Progressive institutionalized real-time disaster management monitoring weather and internal systems to pre-position claim response teams; 24/7 Immediate Response service continued as the core service differentiator.
Progressive monitored each program to ensure rates were adjusted promptly to sustain margins approaching the 4% target, while continuing to cautiously expand standard and preferred auto volume.
